source.language_note: Rulebook is Japanese; dimensions restated in SI.

full_prompt: Design a single-part aluminium scissor-lift link for a technical-college robotics benchmark robot. The link is one of four identical members in a 2-stage scissor pantograph that lifts a 5 kg task payload from 0 to 600 mm above the robot chassis within the technical-college robotics benchmark weight and envelope budget (robot stowed envelope 1000 x 1000 x 500 mm, total robot mass <= 50 kg). The link is a straight aluminium bar of length 400 mm (pin-to-pin center distance) with a cross-section that the designer chooses but must fit within a 25 mm x 12 mm bounding rectangle. The two pin holes at each end have diameter 8.0 mm (H7 fit on an 8 g6 steel pin) and there is one central pivot hole of diameter 8.0 mm at the midpoint. Material is 7075-T6 aluminium with Young's modulus 71.7 GPa, Poisson's ratio 0.33, density 2810 kg/m^3, yield strength 503 MPa, ultimate tensile strength 572 MPa. Load cases: LC1 (Full payload at full extension) — end pin on one side fully fixed, opposite end pin loaded with a tangential 245 N (1/2 of 5 kg x 9.81 m/s^2 x 10 lift ratio due to mechanical disadvantage at full extension); center pivot free. LC2 (Compressive buckling) — axial compressive force of 600 N along the link's long axis (central pin to end pin), worst case during mid-stroke; LC3 (Transverse impact) — 3 g lateral shock (roughly 15 N at the center pivot) simultaneous with LC1 loading. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises under each load case must stay below 335 MPa (yield / 1.5); (R2) linear buckling eigenvalue under LC2 must have a first mode load factor >= 4.0; (R3) max deflection at the center pivot under LC1 must stay below 1.5 mm so the pantograph geometry does not bind; (R4) link mass must be less than 60 g so the 4-link set stays below the 0.5% robot mass budget of 250 g; (R5) fatigue check by peak-stress-vs-S-N: peak stress under LC1 must stay below 180 MPa so 1e6 cycles of lift is achievable for 7075-T6 (Basquin constant). Primary verification: linear static structural FEA of LC1, LC2, LC3 plus linear buckling extraction for LC2. Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are explicitly not required. Output one STEP AP242 file and a report listing link mass in g, max von Mises per case in MPa, center pivot deflection under LC1, and buckling load factor.
short_prompt:
Design a single-part aluminum scissor-lift link: one of four identical members in a 2-stage pantograph lifting a 5 kg payload from 0 to 600 mm above a technical-college robotics benchmark robot chassis. Robot stowed 1000 x 1000 x 500 mm, total robot mass <= 50 kg.

Envelope: straight 400 mm pin-to-pin bar, cross-section within a 25 x 12 mm bounding box; two 8.0 mm end holes (H7/g6 on 8 mm steel pins) and one 8.0 mm central pivot; 7075-T6 aluminum.

Factor regime: yield FoS 1.5, linear buckling eigenvalue >= 4.0 on the axial compression case; S-N 1e6-cycle peak-stress limit 180 MPa for 7075-T6.

Load cases.
- Full payload at full extension: 245 N tangential at one end pin (opposite end fixed, center free), reflecting 10:1 mechanical disadvantage at full stroke.
- Compressive buckling: 600 N axial along the link.
- Transverse impact: 15 N lateral at center pivot simultaneous with the LC1 load.

prompt.title: technical-college robotics benchmark scissor-lift link
prompt.brief: 7075-T6 aluminium scissor-lift link, 400 mm pin-to-pin, sized to the technical-college robotics benchmark 50 kg / 1 m envelope budget with explicit tension, buckling, and fatigue limits.
